task.name: Stop Signal Task (RDoC)
reference_literature: Logan, G. D., & Cowan, W. B. (1984). On the ability to inhibit thought and action: A theory of an act of control. Psychological Review, 91(3), 295-327.
Verbruggen, F., & Logan, G. D. (2008). Response inhibition in the stop-signal paradigm. Trends in Cognitive Sciences, 12(11), 418-424.
Band, G. P. H., van der Molen, M. W., & Logan, G. D. (2003). Horse-race model simulations of the stop-signal procedure. Acta Psychologica, 112(2), 105-142.
Congdon, E., et al. (2012). Measurement and reliability of response inhibition. Frontiers in Psychology, 3, 37.
Verbruggen, F., et al. (2019). A consensus guide to capturing the ability to inhibit actions and impulsive behaviors in the stop-signal task. eLife, 8, e46323.

stimuli.description: Stop signal (star) appearing around the shape on stop trials - detected via the SS div element appended to display_element
Go stimulus (circle or square shape) requiring a key press response

response.response_key_js: (function(){ var img = document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus img.center'); if(!img) return null; var src = img.getAttribute('src') || ''; if(src.indexOf('circle') !== -1) return possibleResponses[0][1]; if(src.indexOf('square') !== -1) return possibleResponses[1][1]; return null; })()

stimuli.description: Attention check question displayed between test blocks

response.response_key_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); if(!el) return null; var text = el.textContent.toLowerCase(); if(text.indexOf('press the q key') !== -1) return 'q'; if(text.indexOf('press the p key') !== -1) return 'p'; if(text.indexOf('press the r key') !== -1) return 'r'; if(text.indexOf('press the s key') !== -1) return 's'; if(text.indexOf('press the t key') !== -1) return 't'; if(text.indexOf('press the j key') !== -1) return 'j'; if(text.indexOf('press the k key') !== -1) return 'k'; if(text.indexOf('press the e key') !== -1) return 'e'; if(text.indexOf('press the m key') !== -1) return 'm'; if(text.indexOf('press the i key') !== -1) return 'i'; if(text.indexOf('press the u key') !== -1) return 'u'; if(text.indexOf('first letter') !== -1) return 'a'; if(text.indexOf('second letter') !== -1) return 'b'; if(text.indexOf('third letter of') !== -1) return 'c'; if(text.indexOf('third to last') !== -1) return 'x'; if(text.indexOf('second to last') !== -1) return 'y'; if(text.indexOf('last letter') !== -1) return 'z'; return null; })()

autocorrelation.rationale: Modest lag-1 autocorrelation is consistently observed in speeded RT tasks (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2004). In stop-signal tasks, participants show trial-to-trial RT carry-over on go trials.

fatigue_drift.rationale: With 180 test trials plus practice, a very mild fatigue drift is expected. Online participants in 10-minute sessions show slight slowing (~0.1-0.2 ms/trial; Fortenbaugh et al., 2015).

post_error_slowing.rationale: Post-error slowing is well-documented in speeded RT tasks including stop-signal tasks (Dutilh et al., 2012; Verbruggen & Logan, 2008). After incorrect go responses, participants slow by 20-80 ms.

condition_repetition.rationale: In the stop-signal task, condition (go vs stop) repetition effects are confounded with the staircase and post-stop slowing. Disabling to avoid double-counting with post_interrupt_slowing.

pink_noise.rationale: Long-range 1/f fluctuations are a signature of human RT time series (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2005). Hurst ~0.75 is typical for choice RT tasks.

post_interrupt_slowing.rationale: Post-stop slowing is well-documented: after successful stop trials, participants slow their go RT on the following trial by ~15-60ms (Verbruggen et al., 2008; Bissett & Logan, 2011).

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Stop-signal task shows substantial individual differences in go RT (SD ~40-60ms across participants; Congdon et al., 2012). SSRT individual differences are partly captured by the staircase. Online samples add additional variability due to hardware/environmental differences.

pilot.rationale: Practice block has 6 trials with 2/3 go and 1/3 stop ratio (from stopSignalsConditions=['go','go','stop'] x shapes=['circle','square'] = 6 unique combos). All conditions should appear within the first practice block. Using #jspsych-content as confirmed in DOM snapshot.

phase_detection.feedback: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus'); if(!el) return false; var text = el.textContent || ''; return (text.indexOf('enter') !== -1 || text.indexOf('Enter') !== -1 || text.indexOf('begin') !== -1 || text.indexOf('completed') !== -1 || text.indexOf('practice') !== -1 || text.indexOf('test portion') !== -1 || text.indexOf('Done') !== -1 || text.indexOf('Welcome') !== -1) && !document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus'); })()

timing.trial_context_js: (function(){ var img = document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus img.center'); if(!img) return ''; var src = img.getAttribute('src') || ''; var shape = src.indexOf('circle') !== -1 ? 'circle' : (src.indexOf('square') !== -1 ? 'square' : 'unknown'); var ss = document.querySelector('div#jspsych-stop-signal-SS'); return shape + (ss ? '_stop' : '_go'); })()

attention_check.response_js: (function(){ var el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); if(!el) return null; var text = el.textContent.toLowerCase(); if(text.indexOf('press the q key') !== -1) return 'q'; if(text.indexOf('press the p key') !== -1) return 'p'; if(text.indexOf('press the r key') !== -1) return 'r'; if(text.indexOf('press the s key') !== -1) return 's'; if(text.indexOf('press the t key') !== -1) return 't'; if(text.indexOf('press the j key') !== -1) return 'j'; if(text.indexOf('press the k key') !== -1) return 'k'; if(text.indexOf('press the e key') !== -1) return 'e'; if(text.indexOf('press the m key') !== -1) return 'm'; if(text.indexOf('press the i key') !== -1) return 'i'; if(text.indexOf('press the u key') !== -1) return 'u'; if(text.indexOf('first letter') !== -1) return 'a'; if(text.indexOf('second letter') !== -1) return 'b'; if(text.indexOf('third letter of') !== -1) return 'c'; if(text.indexOf('third to last') !== -1) return 'x'; if(text.indexOf('second to last') !== -1) return 'y'; if(text.indexOf('last letter') !== -1) return 'z'; return null; })()
